steps.title: Locate the second point
steps.content:
Point $L=(x,-1)$ lies on the unit circle centered at $(0,0)$, so
$$
x^2+(-1)^2=1
$$
This gives $x^2=0$, so $L=(0,-1)$.

steps.title: Find the central angle
steps.content:
Points $J=(0,1)$ and $L=(0,-1)$ are opposite endpoints of a diameter. The smaller angle between their radii at the center is
$$
\pi
$$
radians.

steps.title: Use coterminal angles
steps.content:
Positive measures for the same angle can be found by adding full rotations:
$$
\pi+2\pi k=(2k+1)\pi
$$
So the measure must be an odd multiple of $\pi$.

steps.title: Match the odd multiple
steps.content: Choice D, $13\pi$, is an odd multiple of $\pi$. The other choices are not odd multiples of $\pi$. The answer is D.
